en.title: Response Loop
en.prompt:
Your task is to automatically take turns asking and answering questions. We’ll begin with an initial question. Then go on answering and asking in this pattern:
Question: The initial question
Answer: The answer to the initial question
Question: The question about the reason for the previous answer
Answer: The answer to the previous question
Question:
Keep asking about the reason for the last answer. Stop only when the answer is "That's the way it is" or "We don't know for now". Each question and answer should be a single sentence with no more than 20 words. Add "Q: " before each question and "A: " before each answer.
Ask and answer in "English" regardless of the language I use. Don’t show the translation process. Just write questions and answers in the destination language.
Now, the initial question is: [initial question for the loop]
en.remark: Continuously pose questions revolving around a particular issue to deepen the understanding of the problem. Contributed by @hkfrank996.

related.title: Deep thinking assistant
related.remark: Provide high-quality and valuable questions based on keywords, themes, or concepts, covering various aspects of human cognition, emotions, and behaviors, in order to train one's ability for deep thinking. The response structure of this prompt is very clear, making it suitable for use when organizing concepts. Contributed by @MoeACG.

related.title: Think Tank
related.remark: Provide you with various different perspectives for thinking. Contributed by @jiuwen624.

related.title: Question Assistant
related.remark: Ask from multiple angles to trigger deep thinking. Contributed by @meishiwanwan.

metaDescription: Deepen understanding? Continuously ask 'Why?' to explore the root cause of an issue. Use this recursive Q&A pattern to gain insight.

faq.q: Will the questioning loop drift further and further off topic?
faq.a: Yes. After several rounds the AI may question its way from "why" to philosophical boundaries. Append "every 5 rounds return to the core topic for a stage summary, then continue." Without this "return point," loops diverge endlessly and everyone forgets the original question.

faq.q: What kinds of questions fit this prompt?
faq.a: Questions with real exploratory value ("why do we procrastinate," "what is a good life"). Mechanical questions ("how to reinstall the OS") go off the rails because the answer doesn't need philosophical probing. Just look up tool questions directly.
